title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Bella Goddard informs Inspector Corradino that her sister-in-law, Sonya, had a rift with Randall Goddard and eventually moved to America with her twin children, Pip and Emma. Bella explains that she had suggested Randall include Sonya's children in his will to ensure they would inherit his property if Bella passed away before Sonya. Corradino suspects that these twins, who may be living in Chipping Cheddar, might be attempting to secure the inheritance by tracking the family's wealth and the status of Letitia Blacklock. As Bella grows tired, she urgently pleads with Corradino to protect Blanche, warning him that she is in danger.

After leaving the room, Corradino is informed by Nurse MacLane that Bella's personal documents and photographs were destroyed during the Blitz in London. Corradino continues to contemplate the motives of the twins, theorizing they could be penniless individuals using false identities to target the Blacklock estate.

Meanwhile, at a small estate, Letitia Blacklock instructs the cook, Midge, to prepare various sandwiches and a specialty cake for Dora Bunner's birthday celebration. Midge, a Central European, expresses anxiety about the house and discusses the ingredients for a heavy, chocolate-frosted cake. Midge is superstitious regarding the word "die," following a comment by Mr. Patrick that her cake was the "death of deliciousness," but Letitia reassures her. In the hallway, Letitia prevents Dora from entering the kitchen to avoid disturbing Midge's mood. Dora mentions that Edmund Swithinham called to wish her a happy birthday and intends to bring honey as a gift.
